description: Wishmax Landing Page (Marketing Website) - Next.js + TypeScript + Tailwind marketing site that communicates the hook, proves results with real before/after transformations, and converts visitors into waitlist signups (swappable to App Store install at launch). Consumes PRD-01 design tokens.

userStories.title: Next.js + Tailwind project scaffold with design tokens
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the web project scaffolded and wired to the design system so all pages start on-brand.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js (App Router) + TypeScript project created in a clear web directory
Tailwind configured to consume the exported PRD-01 design tokens (colors, type, spacing, radius) from design/tokens.css — no hardcoded visual values
Brand fonts loaded and applied
Base layout renders a tokenized light / white-canvas page per DESIGN.md with no default-framework boilerplate styling left over
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Done 2026-07-01. Next.js 16 (App Router, Turbopack) + React 19 + TS + Tailwind v4 scaffolded at web/. globals.css @imports ../../design/fonts.css + ../../design/tokens.css (single source of truth, no token copies); Turbopack root set to repo root so the parent-dir imports resolve. Fonts verified loaded (document.fonts.check Inter + Space Grotesk = true). Typecheck, eslint, next build all pass. Browser-verified: white canvas, Space Grotesk 48px black H1, black CTA (backgroundImage:none), no boilerplate.

userStories.title: Global layout, nav & footer
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want consistent header/footer navigation so I can orient and find the CTA anywhere.
acceptanceCriteria: Sticky header with logo/wordmark and a primary CTA button; header stays pinned on scroll and uses design tokens (no hardcoded values)
Footer with secondary links (privacy, terms, contact, social) — placeholder hrefs allowed but present
Fully responsive across mobile, tablet, desktop; mobile nav works
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Done 2026-07-01. Sticky <SiteHeader> (client, mobile menu toggle) + <SiteFooter> rendered in root layout around {children}. Header: Wishmax wordmark (Inter 700, not Space Grotesk <26px) + desktop nav (How it works / Examples / FAQ) + black 'Get my photos' CTA; collapses to a hamburger-toggled stacked menu ≤767px. Footer: brand+tagline and Product/Company/Social link columns (Privacy, Terms, Contact mailto, Instagram/TikTok/X placeholder hrefs) + legal strip. Ported the PRD-01 Button (+ new ButtonLink for anchor CTAs) and the .wm-btn CSS slice into web/app/components/chrome.css (token-only, real pseudo-states). Browser-verified across 390/768/1280: header position:sticky top:0 z:50 white bg, boxShadow none (DESIGN invariant), silver hairline border, stays pinned after 600px scroll; desktop nav shows ≥768px, hamburger ≤767px; mobile menu opens (aria-expanded), CTA black bg backgroundImage:none, closes on link click; 10 footer links present. Typecheck, lint, build all pass; token-literal audit clean.

userStories.title: Hero section with hook + before/after visual
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to instantly understand the value via a strong headline and a real transformation visual.
acceptanceCriteria: Headline communicates the core hook plus a supporting subhead naming the pain (your photos cost you matches) and the promise (pro dating photos from your own face, no photoshoot)
Primary CTA is above the fold
A before/after transformation visual using the PRD-01 before/after pattern (not generic stock)
Looks great on mobile first; no layout shift on load (LCP image reserved/preloaded)

Lint passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Done 2026-07-01. Hero (web/app/components/Hero.tsx + hero.css) leads with the Space Grotesk hook headline (36px→48px), eyebrow Badge, subhead naming the pain (your photos are costing you matches) + promise (pro dating photos from your own face, no photoshoot), above-the-fold black CTA (Get my photos → #waitlist) + ghost CTA (See the transformations → #examples), and the PRD-01 Before/After card as proof. Ported Badge + FeedPhotoTile (rewired to next/image fill + priority/sizes, non-interactive) + BeforeAfterCard into web/app/components/ (+ showcase.css slice). Real photographic-register raster stand-ins in web/public/hero/ generated by web/scripts/gen-hero-images.mjs (sharp SVG→JPG; swappable to licensed photos at same paths, no code change) — NOT gradient placeholders; honest 'Sample transformation' caption. Mobile-first single column → 2-col at ≥768px on the periwinkle wash surface. No CLS (fixed 3:4 aspect-ratio); LCP images priority → <link rel=preload as=image> for both tiles in SSR. Typecheck, lint, build all pass; token-literal audit CLEAN. Browser-verified 390/768/1280.

userStories.title: "How it works" section (3 steps)
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see how simple it is so I trust I can get a result fast.
acceptanceCriteria: Three clear steps (e.g. 1. Upload a few selfies, 2. Pick photos you love, 3. Get profile-ready shots)
Each step has an icon/visual and one tight sentence
Responsive layout using design tokens
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.notes: Done 2026-07-01. HowItWorks section (web/app/components/HowItWorks.tsx + howitworks.css, rendered by page.tsx after Hero) with id='how-it-works' matching the header/footer nav anchors. Three steps as an <ol>: 1. Upload a few selfies, 2. Pick photos you love, 3. Get profile-ready shots — each a Standard Content Card (white surface, --shadow-sm, radius-cards 12px) with a filled mono-weight inline-SVG icon (currentColor ink, 20px per DESIGN.md imagery) on a soft ink-tint chip, a numbered 'Step N' Badge, an Inter-700 title, and ONE tight sentence (2 free-text sizes/card → max-two-type-sizes honored). Section title Inter 700 24px (NOT Space Grotesk — display face is hero-only ≥26px). Mobile-first single column → repeat(3,1fr) at ≥768px. Section on the white canvas (box-shadow none — elevation is cards-only). Typecheck, lint, build all pass; token-literal audit CLEAN. Browser-verified 390/768/1280 (single col → 3 col, white section no shadow, cards --shadow-sm/12px, 3 icons+badges+titles+sentences).

userStories.notes: Done 2026-07-01. Gallery section (web/app/components/Gallery.tsx + gallery.css, rendered by page.tsx after HowItWorks) with id='examples' matching the header/footer 'Examples' nav anchor + the hero's 'See the transformations' CTA. Eight curated sample result tiles rendered with the ported PRD-01 FeedPhotoTile (THE app feed component → gallery mirrors the app's feel), each with a category chip (Travel/Gym/Professional/Night out ×2 via the tile's category prop → Badge onPhoto over the scrim) to show range. next/image + lazy-load (no priority → all 8 emit loading='lazy', verified none preloaded in SSR); fixed 3:4 aspect-ratio → no CLS/jank (verified aspect 1.333). Real photographic-register raster stand-ins in web/public/gallery/ generated by web/scripts/gen-gallery-images.mjs (sharp SVG→JPG, one palette+lighting per category, varied people; reuses the gen-hero-images.mjs pattern; swappable to licensed photos at same paths, no code change) — NOT gradient placeholders; honest 'Sample transformations' caption. Section on the periwinkle→white gradient wash (surface only), box-shadow none, title Inter 700 24px (not the display face). Grid 2→3→4 columns responsive. Typecheck, lint, build all pass; token-literal audit CLEAN. Browser-verified 390/768/1280.

userStories.notes: Done 2026-07-01. Benefits section (web/app/components/Benefits.tsx + benefits.css, rendered by page.tsx after Gallery) with id='benefits'. Maps FOUR concrete, named pain points → Wishmax outcomes, each benefit → exactly one pain: Bad lighting → 'Studio-grade light on every shot'; No good photos → 'A full lineup of scroll-stopping shots'; Expensive photographers → 'Pro results without the pro price tag'; Low matches → 'Photos that pull more right-swipes'. Each is a DESIGN.md Standard Content Card (white surface, --shadow-sm, radius-cards 12px) with a filled mono-weight inline-SVG icon (sun/gallery/tag/heart, currentColor ink, 20px) on a soft ink-tint chip, the pain named as a strong-tone Badge, an Inter-700 outcome title, and ONE tight sentence of specific, confident brand-voice proof copy (no lorem ipsum) — two free-text sizes/card (title+body), pain rides on the Badge → max-two-type honored. Reuses the established eyebrow-Badge + Inter-700-24px-title + slate-lede section header and the HowItWorks icon-chip pattern. Section on the white canvas (rhythm against the Gallery periwinkle wash), box-shadow none. Grid 1 → 2 → 2 cols (4 benefits as a 2×2). Typecheck, lint, build all pass; token-literal audit CLEAN. Browser-verified 390/768/1280 (white section no shadow, Inter-700 24px title, 4 cards each with icon+pain-Badge+outcome-title+sentence, cards --shadow-sm/12px, grid 1→2→2).

userStories.title: Primary CTA + waitlist email capture
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want one obvious next step so I can install or get notified.
acceptanceCriteria: Pre-launch, the primary CTA captures an email for the waitlist; the CTA target is structured to later swap to an App Store / TestFlight link without re-architecting

Waitlist submissions persist via a Next.js route handler behind a thin store abstraction (single saveWaitlistEmail seam); v1 may use a documented placeholder store swappable to the real provider without touching callers
Validation and success/error states handled
No secrets exposed client-side; submission has a concrete spam guard (per-IP rate limit on the route handler and/or hidden honeypot field), verified to reject rapid/bot submissions

Lint passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
